mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and confidence, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month percentage change in RSAFS over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is maintained at an extremely low level, it signals an aggressively accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, leading to positive equity market performance over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where the FEDFUNDS rate is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment and strong job creation indicates a healthy and expanding labor market. This boosts consumer confidence and spending, which in turn drives corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when both UNRATE is below 7.0% and PAYEMS shows a 3-month increase greater than 100,000 persons.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, a sustained increase in industrial production signals a rebound in manufacturing and overall economic activity. This improvement in supply-side capacity and utilization is bullish for equities as corporate revenues and efficiency improve over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: In identified post-recession recovery periods, positive INDPRO growth (3mo change > 0.5%) does not lead to positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month increase in housing starts indicates growing confidence in the economy and future demand for housing. This stimulates related industries and contributes to overall economic growth, leading to positive equity market sentiment over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of instances where HOUST increases by over 100,000 units month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Moderately low and stable inflation, as measured by CPI, provides a favorable and predictable environment for corporate planning and consumer purchasing power. This supports equity valuations without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns show a negative correlation with CPIAUCSL levels when inflation is below 2.5% (year-over-year) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and potential economic contraction. This leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, negatively impacting S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following a 3-month increase in UNRATE greater than 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: When both GDP growth is robust and retail sales are strong, it confirms broad-based economic strength and high consumer confidence. This combination leads to higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 YoY growth > 2.0% AND RSAFS YoY growth > 3.0%
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when both GDPC1 and RSAFS show year-over-year growth exceeding 2.0% and 3.0% respectively.
